summary: Postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud/repo context to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests installer-identifying data — OS hostname, OS username, git user.email, git reflog committer emails, SSH public-key comment identifiers from ~/.ssh/*.pub, gh CLI login/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, git remote origin URL, GCP project/account IDs, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, parent project name/author/repo, current working directory, and CI provider — and POSTs the payload as JSON to a hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The script is framed as 'anonymous diagnostics/telemetry' but the collected fields are personal and infrastructure identifiers, not anonymous counters, and delivery is to an author-controlled endpoint rather than infrastructure the installer configures. Reading ~/.ssh, ~/.aws/config, ~/.gitconfig, and gh CLI hosts.yml during a silent postinstall constitutes reconnaissance of installer identity and cloud infrastructure regardless of the cover-story label.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
